core_mechanism: the quantum sine-Gordon field theory, a nonlinear (1+1)-dimensional field equation whose excitation spectrum consists of topological solitons (kinks) plus their bound states (breathers), organized by a single coupling parameter
brief_reason: The abstract states the system is represented by the quantum sine-Gordon model and that its observed modes (soliton resonance, breathers, interbreather transitions, two-breather excitation) are all described by sine-Gordon field theory with one adjustable coupling parameter c_s=h/H.
